description.task: Write SQL that demonstrates each of the four ACID properties using a bank transfer scenario.
description.context: ACID stands for Atomicity (all-or-nothing), Consistency (rules always satisfied), Isolation (concurrent transactions don't interfere), Durability (committed data persists).
instructions: Run the BEGIN...COMMIT block to perform the transfer.
Run the SUM query to verify total balance is still 2250.00 (unchanged — Consistency).
Run the final SELECT to confirm Alice has 800 and Bob has 700 (Durability).
Add a comment for each section explaining which ACID property it demonstrates.
description.hint: In a proper database: before transfer total = 1000+500+750 = 2250. After transfer: Alice=800, Bob=700, Carol=750. Total still = 2250. Consistency maintained.

description.task: Write a transaction that simulates a failure mid-way and uses ROLLBACK to undo all changes, demonstrating Atomicity.
instructions: Run the first SELECT to record the initial state.
Run the final SELECT and verify all values are exactly as before — ROLLBACK undid all changes.
Add a comment explaining: what would happen to the second UPDATE if the first one failed without ROLLBACK (partial update problem)?
description.hint: ROLLBACK cancels the entire transaction back to the state when BEGIN was called. Without transactions, a failure mid-way would leave the database in an inconsistent state.
description.expectedOutput:
Before: stock=[100,500,200], reserved=[0,0,0]
After ROLLBACK: stock=[100,500,200], reserved=[0,0,0] — identical

description.task: Read a set of scenarios and write the SQL that fixes each ACID violation.
description.context: Understanding which ACID property is being violated helps diagnose production database issues.
instructions: Run Scenario A transaction to mark order 2 as completed.
Run Scenario B — if any rows return, those violate Consistency (amount must be > 0).
Run Scenario C to see the order status distribution.
Add a comment identifying which ACID property each scenario protects.
description.hint: B returns 0 rows (no violations). C shows: completed:2→3, pending:1, failed:1 after the update.
description.expectedOutput:
After Scenario A: order 2 status = 'completed'
B: 0 rows (no violations)
C: completed:3, pending:1, failed:1
